headline: PM Narendra Modi Inaugurates Elevated Port Corridor in Guwahati, Launches Multiple Waterways Projects with ₹526 Crore Investment in Assam
summaryText:
Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways

PM Narendra Modi Inaugurates Elevated Port Corridor in Guwahati, Launches Multiple Waterways Projects with ₹526 Crore Investment in Assam “Assam is today becoming a model for the new future of our Northeast , Ashtalakshmi; The progress here is giving new momentum to the entire Northeast”: PM ModiUnion Minister Sarbananda Sonowal joins Prime Minister Modi as Bhoomi Poojan performed for ₹188 crore Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel, Dibrugarh₹158 crore for Cruise Terminals at Biswanath Ghat & Neamati on Brahmaputra (NW-2)₹180 Crore Elevated Corridor at Pandu Port bridges the Last-Mile Gap Between River and Road, Putting Northeast's Logistic growth on the Fast Track

Prime Minister Narendra Modi dedicated to the nation a state-of-the-art elevated road corridor connecting Pandu Port in Guwahati to National Highway-27, while also laying the foundation stone for the Cruise Terminal at Biswanath Ghat and performing the Bhoomi Pujan for the Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel, Dibrugarh, and the Cruise Terminal at Neamati. The four projects represent a combined investment of ₹526 crore to boost infrastructure on inland waterways on river Brahmaputra (NW-2), as these projects are being implemented by the Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI) under the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (MoPSW).

The Prime Minister announced that the work of building modern cruise terminals at the famous Neamati Ghat and Biswanath Ghat has also commenced from today. PM Modi emphasised that this is not merely an infrastructure project , it is a step that will give a new direction to tourism and the local economy in Assam. The Prime Minister stated that the current government has not limited tourism to just sightseeing but has viewed it as a major opportunity for employment and development. PM Modi noted that with this very vision, the possibilities of water tourism on the Brahmaputra River are being expanded. The Prime Minister highlighted that once the cruise terminals are built, cruise operations on the Brahmaputra will advance further, and it will become even easier for tourists from India and around the world to reach Assam. PM Modi stated that as cruise tourism grows, new employment opportunities will be created for local youth, artisans and handicraft workers will find new markets, and the income of small shopkeepers, boatmen, and people associated with hotels and transport will also increase. "Tourism in Assam is no longer limited to just travel and sightseeing ,it is becoming a new engine of local development and public prosperity," asserted PM Modi.

The programme was held at Jyoti-Bishnu Auditorium, Guwahati, in the august presence of Assam Governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya, Chief Minister Dr Himanta Biswa Sarma and Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal among other dignitaries.

Prime Minister Modi has consistently recognised the immense potential of inland waterways as an environment-friendly and cost-effective mode for passenger and cargo transport. Under his leadership, a new phase of connectivity and growth has taken shape across the northeastern states through terminals, jetties and waterways-linked infrastructure that is transforming livelihoods and deepening India's Act East Policy commitments.

The Prime Minister stated that Assam is today emerging as a model for the new future of India's North East , the Ashtalakshmi. PM Modi noted that the progress of Assam is giving new momentum to the entire North East. Urging every youth and every family, PM Modi asserted, "We must work together for a Viksit Assam so that Assam emerges as a model state in the nation.”

The Prime Minister expressed his gratitude for the privilege of being on the sacred land of Maa Kamakhya on the eve of Navratri. "I have been blessed with the fortune of having your darshan on this pious land of Maa Kamakhya, just before Navratri begins," said PM Modi.

Sarbananda Sonowal said the projects inaugurated and initiated were a direct expression of Prime Minister Modi's vision for a developed and connected Northeast. "Prime Minister Modi ji has done what decades of governance could not — he has turned the Northeast from a region of promise into a region of progress. The Brahmaputra is no longer just a river. Under his leadership, it is becoming a highway of opportunity for millions of people. With his able leadership, the inland waterways are being transformed as the most economical and environment-friendly mode of transportation. Strengthening them has established the path of transformation of India's logistics sector unlocking new growth opportunities,”

The elevated road corridor connecting Pandu Port Complex to NH-27, built with an investment of ₹180 crore, addresses the critical last-mile connectivity gap between one of NW-2's principal river terminals and the national highway network. Engineered to bypass Guwahati's urban congestion, the corridor ensures seamless two-access to Pandu Port, improving operational flexibility and port connectivity reducing logistic cost considerably.

The Cruise Terminal at Biswanath Ghat, for which the foundation stone was laid on Friday, is part of a coordinated effort to build modern cruise infrastructure along the Brahmaputra (NW-2). The terminal will enhance passenger amenities, support river cruise operations and generate fresh economic opportunities for local communities in tourism, hospitality and handicrafts.

The Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel in Dibrugarh, for which the Bhoomi Pujan was performed, is being developed with an investment of ₹188 crore. The first maritime skill development hub of its kind in Northeast India, the RCoE will train over 5,000 students annually in vessel operations, inland navigation and maritime logistics. It will also house research and development infrastructure for crew and trainee programmes, building the skilled manpower base that India's expanding inland waterways sector requires.

The Cruise Terminal at Neamati, also initiated through a Bhoomi Pujan on Friday, will strengthen cruise tourism and organised passenger movement along NW-2, improving infrastructure for tourists and communities along the Brahmaputra corridor. The two cruise terminal projects at Biswanath Ghat and Neamati carry a combined investment of ₹158 crore.

"These projects worth thousands of crores are creating new avenues of employment for Assam's youth and are boosting the state's prosperity," asserted PM Modi. The Prime Minister highlighted that in the past decade, the current government has undertaken a monumental initiative towards self-reliance.

"The Brahmaputra has always been the lifeline of the north-east — but we never truly harnessed its potential as an engine of growth. Our dynamic Prime Minister Modi ji has changed that. Waterways offer cost advantage which directly benefits farmers, traders and manufacturers across the Northeast. The re-energised waterways system in the region strengthens that economic artery at its most critical juncture. The upcoming cruise terminals at Biswanath Ghat and Neamati will act as new livelihood platforms for enriching thousands of families along the river. The Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel ensures this growth is sustainable by building the skilled human capital to expand this ecosystem for the benefit of the next generation. Thousands of our youths will now be able to equip themselves to be a part of the upcoming and booming maritime industry. Under Modiji's Act East Policy, the Brahmaputra is becoming an empowered conduit connecting Assam to Southeast Asia — through a mode of transport that is green, inclusive and sustainable,” said Sarbananda Sonowal.

Together, the four projects are designed to boost trade, tourism, skilling, employment generation, reduce logistic cost and facilitate regional and global connectivity along National Waterway-2, advancing the Government of India's broader vision for developing Brahmaputra as a transformative logistics and tourism corridor for Assam and the wider Northeast.
category: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways
fullText:
Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways

PM Narendra Modi Inaugurates Elevated Port Corridor in Guwahati, Launches Multiple Waterways Projects with ₹526 Crore Investment in Assam “Assam is today becoming a model for the new future of our Northeast , Ashtalakshmi; The progress here is giving new momentum to the entire Northeast”: PM ModiUnion Minister Sarbananda Sonowal joins Prime Minister Modi as Bhoomi Poojan performed for ₹188 crore Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel, Dibrugarh₹158 crore for Cruise Terminals at Biswanath Ghat & Neamati on Brahmaputra (NW-2)₹180 Crore Elevated Corridor at Pandu Port bridges the Last-Mile Gap Between River and Road, Putting Northeast's Logistic growth on the Fast Track

Prime Minister Narendra Modi dedicated to the nation a state-of-the-art elevated road corridor connecting Pandu Port in Guwahati to National Highway-27, while also laying the foundation stone for the Cruise Terminal at Biswanath Ghat and performing the Bhoomi Pujan for the Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel, Dibrugarh, and the Cruise Terminal at Neamati. The four projects represent a combined investment of ₹526 crore to boost infrastructure on inland waterways on river Brahmaputra (NW-2), as these projects are being implemented by the Inland Waterways Authority of India (IWAI) under the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (MoPSW).

The Prime Minister announced that the work of building modern cruise terminals at the famous Neamati Ghat and Biswanath Ghat has also commenced from today. PM Modi emphasised that this is not merely an infrastructure project , it is a step that will give a new direction to tourism and the local economy in Assam. The Prime Minister stated that the current government has not limited tourism to just sightseeing but has viewed it as a major opportunity for employment and development. PM Modi noted that with this very vision, the possibilities of water tourism on the Brahmaputra River are being expanded. The Prime Minister highlighted that once the cruise terminals are built, cruise operations on the Brahmaputra will advance further, and it will become even easier for tourists from India and around the world to reach Assam. PM Modi stated that as cruise tourism grows, new employment opportunities will be created for local youth, artisans and handicraft workers will find new markets, and the income of small shopkeepers, boatmen, and people associated with hotels and transport will also increase. "Tourism in Assam is no longer limited to just travel and sightseeing ,it is becoming a new engine of local development and public prosperity," asserted PM Modi.

The programme was held at Jyoti-Bishnu Auditorium, Guwahati, in the august presence of Assam Governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya, Chief Minister Dr Himanta Biswa Sarma and Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal among other dignitaries.

Prime Minister Modi has consistently recognised the immense potential of inland waterways as an environment-friendly and cost-effective mode for passenger and cargo transport. Under his leadership, a new phase of connectivity and growth has taken shape across the northeastern states through terminals, jetties and waterways-linked infrastructure that is transforming livelihoods and deepening India's Act East Policy commitments.

The Prime Minister stated that Assam is today emerging as a model for the new future of India's North East , the Ashtalakshmi. PM Modi noted that the progress of Assam is giving new momentum to the entire North East. Urging every youth and every family, PM Modi asserted, "We must work together for a Viksit Assam so that Assam emerges as a model state in the nation.”

The Prime Minister expressed his gratitude for the privilege of being on the sacred land of Maa Kamakhya on the eve of Navratri. "I have been blessed with the fortune of having your darshan on this pious land of Maa Kamakhya, just before Navratri begins," said PM Modi.

Sarbananda Sonowal said the projects inaugurated and initiated were a direct expression of Prime Minister Modi's vision for a developed and connected Northeast. "Prime Minister Modi ji has done what decades of governance could not — he has turned the Northeast from a region of promise into a region of progress. The Brahmaputra is no longer just a river. Under his leadership, it is becoming a highway of opportunity for millions of people. With his able leadership, the inland waterways are being transformed as the most economical and environment-friendly mode of transportation. Strengthening them has established the path of transformation of India's logistics sector unlocking new growth opportunities,”

The elevated road corridor connecting Pandu Port Complex to NH-27, built with an investment of ₹180 crore, addresses the critical last-mile connectivity gap between one of NW-2's principal river terminals and the national highway network. Engineered to bypass Guwahati's urban congestion, the corridor ensures seamless two-access to Pandu Port, improving operational flexibility and port connectivity reducing logistic cost considerably.

The Cruise Terminal at Biswanath Ghat, for which the foundation stone was laid on Friday, is part of a coordinated effort to build modern cruise infrastructure along the Brahmaputra (NW-2). The terminal will enhance passenger amenities, support river cruise operations and generate fresh economic opportunities for local communities in tourism, hospitality and handicrafts.

The Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel in Dibrugarh, for which the Bhoomi Pujan was performed, is being developed with an investment of ₹188 crore. The first maritime skill development hub of its kind in Northeast India, the RCoE will train over 5,000 students annually in vessel operations, inland navigation and maritime logistics. It will also house research and development infrastructure for crew and trainee programmes, building the skilled manpower base that India's expanding inland waterways sector requires.

The Cruise Terminal at Neamati, also initiated through a Bhoomi Pujan on Friday, will strengthen cruise tourism and organised passenger movement along NW-2, improving infrastructure for tourists and communities along the Brahmaputra corridor. The two cruise terminal projects at Biswanath Ghat and Neamati carry a combined investment of ₹158 crore.

"These projects worth thousands of crores are creating new avenues of employment for Assam's youth and are boosting the state's prosperity," asserted PM Modi. The Prime Minister highlighted that in the past decade, the current government has undertaken a monumental initiative towards self-reliance.

"The Brahmaputra has always been the lifeline of the north-east — but we never truly harnessed its potential as an engine of growth. Our dynamic Prime Minister Modi ji has changed that. Waterways offer cost advantage which directly benefits farmers, traders and manufacturers across the Northeast. The re-energised waterways system in the region strengthens that economic artery at its most critical juncture. The upcoming cruise terminals at Biswanath Ghat and Neamati will act as new livelihood platforms for enriching thousands of families along the river. The Regional Centre of Excellence (RCoE) at Bogibeel ensures this growth is sustainable by building the skilled human capital to expand this ecosystem for the benefit of the next generation. Thousands of our youths will now be able to equip themselves to be a part of the upcoming and booming maritime industry. Under Modiji's Act East Policy, the Brahmaputra is becoming an empowered conduit connecting Assam to Southeast Asia — through a mode of transport that is green, inclusive and sustainable,” said Sarbananda Sonowal.

Together, the four projects are designed to boost trade, tourism, skilling, employment generation, reduce logistic cost and facilitate regional and global connectivity along National Waterway-2, advancing the Government of India's broader vision for developing Brahmaputra as a transformative logistics and tourism corridor for Assam and the wider Northeast.

headline: Accounts & Audit Module Launched on UMEED Central Portal to Strengthen Financial Governance of Waqf Institutions
summaryText:
**UPSC Study Note: Ministry of Minority Affairs' UMEED Central Portal**

**1. CONTEXT:**
The Ministry of Minority Affairs has launched the Accounts & Audit Module on the Unified Waqf Management, Empowerment, Efficiency and Development (UMEED) Central Portal. This initiative aims to strengthen financial governance and transparency in the management of Waqf institutions across the country.

**2. KEY FEATURES:**

* The module provides a digital framework for recording and managing financial transactions of Waqf institutions.
* It enables the capture of income and expenditure details of Waqf properties and institutions.
* Automated calculation and payment of statutory contributions to State Waqf Boards through the portal.
* Supports the collection of funds for the welfare and development of intended beneficiaries (poor, women, orphans) of Waqf properties.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE:**
The launch of the Accounts & Audit Module is a significant step towards:
* Strengthening financial governance and oversight in Waqf institutions.
* Ensuring transparency and accountability in the management of Waqf properties.
* Efficient monitoring of financial activities related to Waqf institutions.
* Timely collection of funds for the welfare and development of intended beneficiaries.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE:**
This initiative is relevant to UPSC aspirants in the context of:
* Government initiatives for minority welfare and development.
* E-governance and digital transformation in public institutions.
* Financial management and accountability in public sector organizations.
* Institutional governance and management of public assets.
This study note can be a valuable resource for students preparing for the UPSC Civil Services Examination, particularly in the context of paper 4 - General Studies (Governance and Public Policy).
fullText:
Ministry of Minority Affairs

Accounts & Audit Module Launched on UMEED Central Portal to Strengthen Financial Governance of Waqf Institutions

In a significant step towards strengthening transparency and accountability in the management of Waqf institutions, the Accounts & Audit Module on the UMEED Central Portal (2025) was launched today by Dr. Chandra Shekhar Kumar, Secretary, Ministry of Minority Affairs.

The newly launched module provides a structured digital framework for recording and managing the financial transactions of Waqf institutions across the country. It enables systematic capture of details related to income and expenditure of Waqf properties and institutions, thereby ensuring proper maintenance of financial records.

A key feature of the module is the provision for automated calculation and payment of statutory contributions payable to the respective State Waqf Boards through the portal itself. This digital mechanism is expected to facilitate greater transparency, improve compliance with statutory requirements and enable efficient monitoring of financial activities related to Waqf institutions.

The initiative will also support the timely collection of funds that are meant for the welfare and development of the poor, women and orphans who are the intended beneficiaries of Waqf properties.

With the launch of the Accounts & Audit Module, the UMEED Central Portal continues to evolve as a comprehensive digital platform for the effective administration and management of Waqf assets in the country. The module is expected to significantly strengthen financial governance and oversight in the functioning of Waqf institutions.

The Ministry of Minority Affairs has been continuously working to ensure the smooth implementation of the Unified Waqf Management, Empowerment, Efficiency and Development (UMEED) Central Portal. From conducting regular review meetings with State Waqf Boards to organising training sessions, setting up help desks and providing continuous technical support, the Ministry has been working tirelessly to facilitate the timely upload and effective management of Waqf properties on the portal.

Officials of the Ministry noted that the teams involved in the initiative have been “burning the midnight oil” to ensure that the digital transformation of Waqf administration through the UMEED portal proceeds smoothly and efficiently.

These sustained efforts are aimed at creating a transparent, accountable and technology-driven system for the management of Waqf properties in the country. The Ministry reiterated its commitment to strengthening institutional governance and ensuring that Waqf assets are managed efficiently so that their benefits reach the intended sections of society.

headline: Union Minister Shri Chirag Paswan inaugurated Incubation Centre under PMFME Scheme at Tezpur University, Assam on 13 March
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT:**
The context of this news article revolves around the inauguration of a Common Incubation Centre under the Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises Scheme (PMFME Scheme) by the Union Minister of Food Processing Industries, Shri Chirag Paswan, at Tezpur University in Assam. The initiative aims to support micro food processing enterprises, Self-Help Groups, Farmer Producer Organizations, cooperatives, startups, and aspiring entrepreneurs.

**2. KEY FEATURES:**
- The Incubation Centre has been established with 100% financial assistance from the Ministry of Food Processing Industries.
- The facility is a modern food processing innovation and entrepreneurship hub, supporting micro food processing enterprises, SHGs, FPOs, cooperatives, startups, and aspiring entrepreneurs.
- The programme includes interaction with PMFME Scheme beneficiaries, entrepreneurs, start-ups, faculty members of Tezpur University, and other stakeholders from the food processing ecosystem.
- The Department of Food Engineering and Technology at Tezpur University will provide technical guidance and mentorship to entrepreneurs.
- The incubation centre has been established at a cost of Rs. 2.35 crore, with financial assistance from the Ministry of Food Processing Industries.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE:**
- The initiative aligns with the Government of India's vision to strengthen the food processing ecosystem in the North-Eastern Region through innovation, entrepreneurship, and value addition to agricultural produce.
- The programme is expected to serve Sonitpur and neighbouring districts, creating a regional hub for food processing enterprise development.
- It aims to reduce food wastage, improve value chains, and support the Prime Minister's vision of doubling farmers' income.
- The facility will also promote regional food products, enhance production, quality, and exports, ultimately emerging as a "Global Food Basket".

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE:**
- The initiative is a key component of the Government of India's Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan, aiming to promote self-reliance and economic growth.
- It demonstrates the Government's commitment to strengthening the food processing sector, creating employment opportunities, and promoting regional economic growth.
- The programme also emphasizes the importance of collaboration between government, academia, and entrepreneurs for building a vibrant food processing sector.
- As a part of the PMFME Scheme, the initiative promotes entrepreneurship and innovation in the food processing sector, aligning with the UPSC's focus on economic development and self-reliance.
fullText:
Ministry of Food Processing Industries

Union Minister Shri Chirag Paswan inaugurated Incubation Centre under PMFME Scheme at Tezpur University, Assam on 13 March

The Union Minister for Food Processing Industries, Shri. Chirag Paswan, inaugurated a state-of-the-art Common Incubation Centre at Tezpur University in Sonitpur district of Assam on 13 March, 2026 with 100% financial assistance from the Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI), Government of India, under the Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises Scheme (PMFME Scheme).

The incubation centre has been established as a modern food processing innovation and entrepreneurship hub aimed at supporting micro food processing enterprises, Self-Help Groups (SHGs), Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs), cooperatives, startups and aspiring entrepreneurs.

The programme commenced with inauguration of the PMFME Incubation Centre by the Minister in the august presence of Shri Prithiraj Rabha, MLA of Tezpur constituency, Shri D. Praveen, Joint Secretary, Ministry of Food Processing Industries, Prof. Amarendra Kumar Das, Pro-Vice Chancellor, Tezpur University, Shri Megha Nidhi Dahal, MD, AIDC, Shri Sazzad Alam, Additional Secretary, Govt. of Assam and Shri Ananda Kumar Das, District Commissioner. This was followed by the interaction of the Minister with PMFME Scheme beneficiaries, entrepreneurs, start-ups, faculty members of Tezpur University and other stakeholders from the food processing ecosystem.

In his welcome address, Prof. Amarendra Kumar Das, Pro-Vice Chancellor, Tezpur University while highlighting the significance of the initiative expressed his gratitude to the Ministry of Food Processing Industries for providing the necessary assistance. He also informed that the Department of Food Engineering and Technology at Tezpur University will provide technical guidance and mentorship to entrepreneurs in areas such as processing technologies, packaging, branding, labelling and compliance with food safety standards

During his opening remarks, Shri D. Praveen, Joint Secretary, MoFPI highlighted the importance of collaboration between government, academia, and entrepreneurs for building a vibrant food processing sector. He also informed that Ministry of Food Processing Industries has given a financial assistance to the tune of Rs. 2.35 crore for the incubation centre.

Shri Prithiraj Rabha, MLA of Tezpur constituency, in his remarks, encouraged university students to take up food processing and skill development courses to tap emerging opportunities in the sector. He urged the youth to become tomorrow’s entrepreneurs and contribute to regional economic growth and employment generation.

Shri Chirag Paswan, Union Minister of Food Processing Industries, in his inaugural address highlighted the Government of India’s vision to strengthen the food processing ecosystem in the North-Eastern Region through innovation, entrepreneurship and value addition to agricultural produce.

Encouraging youth to become job creators instead of job seekers, the Minister emphasised the growing opportunities in the food processing sector, where many professionals are now taking up entrepreneurial ventures. He appreciated the University for establishing seven specialised processing and support lines for fruits, grains, spices, bakery and other segments, which will help entrepreneurs develop and commercialise new products. The facility is expected to serve Sonitpur and neighbouring districts including Darrang, Udalguri, Biswanath, Nagaon and Golaghat, creating a regional hub for food processing enterprise development.

Highlighting the impact of the PMFME Scheme under the Aatmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan, Shri Paswan noted that loans have been sanctioned to over 1.87 lakh micro food processing enterprises across the country, benefiting FPOs, SHGs, cooperatives and farmers through financial assistance, capacity building and market linkages.

He stressed the importance of a “Farm to Fork” approach to reduce food wastage, improve value chains and support the Prime Minister’s vision of doubling farmers’ income. The Minister said India has the potential to emerge as a “Global Food Basket” through enhanced production, quality, exports and promotion of regional food products.

Describing Assam as the gateway to the North-East, he reiterated the Government’s focus on strengthening food processing infrastructure in the region.

Expressing his vision, Shri Paswan stated, “I want to see ‘Made in India’ food products on each and every dining table across the world.”

headline: Union Minister of Coal and Mines Shri G Kishan Reddy to Perform Bhoomi Pujan Ceremony for two Integrated Coal Gasification Plants in Bhadrawati, Maharashtra
summaryText:
**Topic: Union Minister of Coal and Mines Shri G Kishan Reddy to Perform Bhoomi Pujan Ceremony for two Integrated Coal Gasification Plants in Bhadrawati, Maharashtra**

**1. CONTEXT: (approx. 20 words)**

The Union Minister of Coal and Mines, Shri G Kishan Reddy, is scheduled to perform the Bhoomi Pujan ceremony for two integrated coal gasification plants in Bhadrawati, Maharashtra.

**2. KEY FEATURES: (approx. 40 words)**

- The coal gasification mission aims to gasify 100 million tonnes of coal by 2030.
- The government has launched a financial incentive scheme of Rs 8500 crores for promoting coal gasification.
- Two private entities, Greta Energy and Metal Private Limited, and New Era Cleantech Solution Pvt. Ltd., will set up coal gasification projects for Direct Reduced Iron (DRI) and ammonium nitrate and hydrogen production, respectively.
- The projects will enable import substitution for urea, natural gas, ethanol, and coking coal.
- The coal gasification mission promotes cleaner energy solutions and sustainable coal use.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE: (approx. 40 words)**

The launch of the coal gasification mission and the financial incentive scheme will accelerate coal resource utilization, promoting cleaner energy solutions and sustainable coal use. This initiative will also enhance energy security and reduce reliance on imported fuels.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE: (approx. 40 words)**

- This topic is relevant to the Indian economy and energy sector.
- The coal gasification mission and financial incentive scheme align with the government's focus on energy security, cleaner energy solutions, and sustainable resource utilization.
- The topic can be linked to the UPSC syllabus, specifically to the topics of energy policy, coal and mineral resources, and sustainable development.
fullText:
Union Minister of Coal and Mines Shri G Kishan Reddy to Perform Bhoomi Pujan Ceremony for two Integrated Coal Gasification Plants in Bhadrawati, Maharashtra

Union Minister of Coal & Mines Shri G Kishan Reddy is on visit to Western Coalfields Limited, Nagpur on 13th and 14th March, 2026. During his visit, Shri Reddy will perform the Bhoomi Pujan Ceremony of two integrated Coal Gasification Plants. The Minister will also conduct a review of Murpar Mine Closure site and will hold a Mine Closure Advisory Committee meeting with stakeholders.

The Coal Gasification Mission was launched, with the aim of gasifying 100 million tonnes of coal by 2030, thereby maximizing the value and utility of this vital resource and also embracing cleaner energy solutions and ensuring the sustainable use of coal.

A financial incentive scheme of Rs 8500 crores was launched in January 2024 for promotion of coal gasification in the country. This initiative will lead to import substitution for urea, natural gas, ethanol, coking coal etc. It will also accelerate coal resource utilisation.

The vision laid out for Coal Gasification is coming to fruition as Union Minister of Coal and Mines Shri G Kishan Reddy along with the Chief Minister of Maharashtra, Shri Devendra Fadnavis will perform a Bhoomi Pujan Ceremony and lay the foundation stone of 2 private entities - Greta Energy and Metal Private Limited and New Era Cleantech Solution Pvt. Ltd- in Bhadrawati Industrial Area, Chandrapur for the projects. These projects are among the seven projects that have been selected for grant of financial incentive under GOI scheme.

Greta Energy and Metal Private Limited is setting up a coal gasification project for Direct Reduced Iron (DRI) production while New Era Cleantech Solution Pvt. Ltd. is setting up a coal gasification project for production of ammonium nitrate and hydrogen.

In 2020, the Coal Gasification Mission was launched, with the aim of gasifying 100 million tonnes of coal by 2030, thereby maximizing the value and utility of this vital resource and also embracing cleaner energy solutions and ensuring the sustainable use of coal.Aligning with… https://t.co/alkWVpUlOZ

headline: RASHTRAPATI BHAVAN HOSTS ‘PURPLE FEST’
summaryText:
**Rashtrapati Bhavan's Purple Fest: A Celebration of Inclusion and Empowerment**

**1. CONTEXT**
On March 13, 2026, Rashtrapati Bhavan hosted the 'Purple Fest', a special celebration aimed at showcasing the talents, achievements, and aspirations of Divyangjan (people with disabilities) in India.

**2. KEY FEATURES**

- Over 8,000 Divyangjan visited Amrit Udyan, which was open exclusively for them.
- Divyangjan participated in various fun games, learning activities, and cultural events through stalls set up by organizations working for them.
- President Droupadi Murmu attended the Fest and witnessed cultural performances by Divyangjan in the Open Air Theatre.
- The event aimed to raise awareness about different disabilities, promote understanding, acceptance, and inclusion of people with disabilities within society.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**
The 'Purple Fest' is a significant initiative to recognize the importance of inclusivity and social justice, as enshrined in the Constitution of India. The event highlights the government's commitment to empowering Divyangjan and making them 'equal partners' in the journey towards 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**
- **Social Justice**: The event showcases the importance of social justice and equality in Indian society.
- **Empowerment of Divyangjan**: The initiative demonstrates the government's efforts to empower Divyangjan and make them an integral part of the country's development process.
- **National Goal**: The 'Purple Fest' highlights the collective goal of building a 'Viksit Bharat' by 2047 and emphasizes the role of Divyangjan in achieving this goal.
- **Constitutional Provisions**: The event illustrates the importance of the Directive Principles of State Policy, particularly those related to the rights of Divyangjan.
- **Inclusive Governance**: The 'Purple Fest' demonstrates the government's commitment to inclusive governance and the need for active participation from individuals and institutions in promoting social inclusion.
fullText:
RASHTRAPATI BHAVAN HOSTS ‘PURPLE FEST’ WE ARE MOVING FORWARD WITH THE COLLECTIVE GOAL OF ‘VIKSIT BHARAT’ BY THE YEAR 2047 AND DIVYANGJAN ARE EQUAL PARTNERS IN THIS JOURNEY: PRESIDENT DROUPADI MURMU

Rashtrapati Bhavan hosted ‘Purple Fest’ today (March 13, 2026) to celebrate the talent, achievements and aspirations of Divyangjan. During day-long celebration, over 8,000 Divyangjan visited Amrit Udyan which was open exclusively for them today. They also participated in several fun games and learning activities through stalls set up by various organisations working for the Divyangjan.

In the evening, the President of India Smt Droupadi Murmu graced the Fest and witnessed cultural performances by Divyangjan at the Open Air Theatre in the President’s Estate.

Speaking on the occasion, the President said that no country or society is defined solely by what it has accomplished for its privileged sections, but by the extent of its sensitivity towards the marginalized sections. If we look at Indian history, we find that sensitivity, inclusivity, and belief in harmony have been the defining elements of our culture and civilization. The Constitution of India provides us with ideal social standards. The Preamble to our Constitution establishes the ideals of social justice, equality of status and dignity of the individual. The Directive Principles of State Policy grant Divyangjan the right to education, work and public assistance.

The President said that the empowerment of Divyangjan is crucial for building an inclusive society. However, this cannot be achieved solely through the efforts of the government. It requires the active participation of every individual and institution in the society.

The President said that we are moving forward with the collective goal of ‘Viksit Bharat’ by the year 2047. Divyangjan are equal partners in this journey. It is the responsibility of every member of society to provide them with the opportunity to progress with equal prospects and dignity.

The President said that Divyangjan should move forward with self-confidence. They will get full support of government and society in their journey. She stated that their dedication, hard work and diligence will not only pave the path of progress for them but will also serve as an inspiration to all fellow citizens.

The ‘Purple Fest’, organised by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India, aims to raise awareness about different disabilities and their impact on people's lives and to promote understanding, acceptance, and inclusion of persons with disabilities within society.

Please click here to see the President's speech-

headline: 12-Day Tribes Art Fest 2026 Concludes in New Delhi; Over ₹1.25 Crore Direct Sales of Tribal Artworks Recorded
summaryText:
**Topic: Ministry of Tribal Affairs 12-Day Tribes Art Fest 2026**

**Section 1: CONTEXT**

The Ministry of Tribal Affairs, under the aegis of the Indian Government, organized the 12-Day Tribes Art Fest 2026 in New Delhi. The festival aimed to showcase India's rich tribal art traditions, cultural heritage, and creativity. The event was held at the Travancore Palace, K.G. Marg, New Delhi, from an unspecified start date to a concluding valedictory ceremony on an unknown day. The festival received enthusiastic participation from tribal artists, contemporary artists, cultural groups, students, and art enthusiasts from across the country.

**Section 2: KEY FEATURES**

Key features of the festival include:

1. **Participation of Tribal Artists**: Over 30 tribal art forms, 70 tribal artists, and more than 1,000 artworks were showcased during the festival.
2. **Collaboration between Tribal and Contemporary Artists**: The festival featured collaborations between tribal and contemporary artists, resulting in unique creative expressions that blended traditional knowledge with modern artistic practices.
3. **Exhibition and Workshops**: Hands-on workshops, live art demonstrations, and interactive sessions enabled visitors to closely engage with tribal art forms and traditions.
4. **Cultural Performances**: Cultural performances by tribal groups from different regions of the country presented traditional music and dance.
5. **Direct Sales of Tribal Artworks**: Over ₹1.25 crore worth of tribal artworks were sold during the festival, directly benefiting the tribal artists.

**Section 3: SIGNIFICANCE**

The significance of the 12-Day Tribes Art Fest 2026 lies in its:

1. **Promotion of Tribal Talent**: The festival provided a platform for tribal artists to showcase their work, thereby promoting their talent and creative expression.
2. **Cultural Exchange and Engagement**: The event facilitated cultural exchange and engagement between tribal communities and visitors from diverse backgrounds.
3. **Livelihood Opportunities**: The direct sales of tribal artworks during the festival contributed to strengthening the livelihoods of tribal artists.
4. **Awareness and Appreciation**: The festival helped raise awareness and appreciation for India's rich tribal heritage among wider audiences.

**Section 4: UPSC RELEVANCE**

The 12-Day Tribes Art Fest 2026 is relevant to the UPSC syllabus in the following ways:

1. **Social Justice and Empowerment**: The festival's focus on promoting tribal talent and livelihood opportunities highlights the importance of social justice and empowerment in tribal communities.
2. **Cultural Exchange and Dialogue**: The cultural performances and collaborations between tribal and contemporary artists underscore the significance of cultural exchange and dialogue in fostering social harmony and understanding.
3. **Government Initiatives**: The festival demonstrates the government's efforts to promote tribal art and culture, which is a key aspect of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs' mandate.
4. **Inclusive Growth**: The event's emphasis on direct sales of tribal artworks and livelihood opportunities highlights the importance of inclusive growth and sustainable development in India's tribal regions.

Key takeaways for the UPSC exam:

* The Ministry of Tribal Affairs' efforts to promote tribal art and culture are critical in preserving India's rich cultural heritage.
* The festival demonstrates the importance of cultural exchange and dialogue in fostering social harmony and understanding.
* The direct sales of tribal artworks during the festival contributed to strengthening the livelihoods of tribal artists, underscoring the significance of inclusive growth and sustainable development in India's tribal regions.
* The government's initiatives in promoting tribal talent and livelihood opportunities are essential in addressing social and economic disparities in tribal communities.
fullText:
Ministry of Tribal Affairs

12-Day Tribes Art Fest 2026 Concludes in New Delhi; Over ₹1.25 Crore Direct Sales of Tribal Artworks Recorded Over 10,000 Visitors Celebrated India’s Tribal Art Heritage; Paves the Way for a New Era of Tribal Art and Creative Collaboration

The Tribes Art Fest 2026, organised by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, concluded today with a grand Valedictory Ceremony at Travancore Palace, K.G. Marg, New Delhi, marking the culmination of a 12-day celebration of tribal art, culture, and creativity. The festival witnessed enthusiastic participation from tribal artists, contemporary artists, cultural groups, students, and art enthusiasts from across the country.

More than 10,000 visitors attended the exhibition, reflecting the growing public interest and appreciation for India’s rich tribal art traditions. The festival served as a vibrant platform for artistic collaboration, cultural exchange, and public engagement, showcasing over 30 tribal art forms, 70 tribal artists, and more than 1,000 artworks. During the exhibition, contemporary artists also collaborated with tribal artists, resulting in unique creative expressions that blended traditional knowledge with modern artistic practices.

The festival also featured hands-on workshops, live art demonstrations, and interactive sessions, enabling visitors to closely engage with tribal art forms and traditions. Cultural performances by tribal groups from different regions of the country further enriched the festival by presenting traditional music and dance.

The valedictory ceremony commenced with the arrival of dignitaries and a walkthrough of the exhibition, followed by the rendition of the National Song Vande Mataram. The event was graced by Shri Jual Oram, Hon’ble Union Minister of Tribal Affairs, as the Chief Guest. Shri Gajendra Singh Shekhawat, Hon’ble Union Minister of Culture and Tourism, Smt. Rekha Gupta, Hon’ble Chief Minister of Delhi, and Shri Durgadas Uikey, Hon’ble Minister of State for Tribal Affairs, attended as Guests of Honour. Ms. Jai Madaan, noted influencer, and Smt. Jyotsna Suri from FICCI were also present on the occasion along with senior officials from the Ministry of Tribal Affairs.

“This has truly been a successful experiment,” said Smt. Ranjana Chopra, Secretary, Ministry of Tribal Affairs. “By bringing together an art gallery ecosystem, corporate buyers, and institutional collaborations, we have created a platform that not only showcases tribal art but also directly strengthens the livelihoods of tribal artists.

The Ministry has been steadily working towards infrastructure development, but we also realised the importance of focusing on livelihoods. In the tribal context, there is a very fine line between livelihood and art and culture, because their art itself is a living expression of their identity and traditions.

This is a moment of great pride for us. In just the past ten days, artworks worth over ₹1.25 crore have been sold. There are artists whose regular monthly income may be around ₹25,000, but through this platform some of them are returning home with earnings of nearly ₹7 lakh. That transformation truly reflects the impact of this initiative.”

Shri Durgadas Uikey, Hon’ble Minister of State for Tribal Affairs, emphasized that tribal art reflects the deep and intrinsic connection of tribal communities with nature, culture, and tradition. He noted that such artistic expressions are not merely aesthetic creations but are deeply rooted in the everyday lives, beliefs, and collective memories of tribal societies. Through motifs, colours, and storytelling traditions, tribal art carries forward centuries-old knowledge systems and cultural values from one generation to the next.

He further stated that initiatives such as the exhibition provide an important platform for promoting tribal talent, encouraging cultural exchange, and expanding awareness about the richness of tribal heritage among wider audiences. By creating opportunities for artists to directly engage with art lovers, collectors, and institutions, such efforts also contribute to strengthening livelihood opportunities for tribal artisans.

He also highlighted that the participation of artists from diverse tribal communities across the country made the exhibition truly representative of India’s rich tribal heritage, bringing together an extraordinary range of traditional and contemporary tribal art forms from different regions.

Speaking on the occasion, Smt. Rekha Gupta, Hon’ble Chief Minister of Delhi, appreciated the efforts of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs in bringing the richness of tribal art and culture to the national capital and providing a valuable platform for artists from different parts of the country. She noted that such initiatives play an important role in highlighting the vibrant traditions, creativity, and cultural heritage of India’s tribal communities, while also enabling citizens and visitors to experience these art forms more closely.

She further remarked that platforms like these help bridge cultural distances by connecting artists directly with audiences, art lovers, and collectors, thereby strengthening appreciation for tribal creativity and craftsmanship. Emphasizing the inclusive spirit of the city, she added that the national capital will always welcome artists and cultural practitioners who enrich Delhi with their traditions, stories, and artistic expressions.

Shri Gajendra Singh Shekhawat, Hon’ble Union Minister of Culture and Tourism appreciated the effort to directly connect artists with audiences, art enthusiasts, and institutions. He noted that such initiatives that bring together artists and communities are both commendable and worthy of emulation. He expressed gratitude to all the artists present who have beautifully portrayed their emotions, cultural heritage, lifestyles, and traditions through their works, whether on paper or through various artistic mediums, and shared them with the wider public.

Emphasizing the deeper significance of tribal art, he said that tribal paintings and other art forms are not merely visual expressions; they are living archives that carry the knowledge, memories, and traditions of tribal societies. Each expression holds meaning, reflecting the artist’s emotions, and every line represents a worldview deeply connected with nature and community life.

He further highlighted that tribal art reflects traditional ecological knowledge, spiritual imagination, and the social stories of communities through diverse artistic forms. Congratulating the artists on the occasion, he remarked that art should not remain merely an object to be viewed or admired but should also serve as a meaningful source of livelihood for artists.

He emphasized that initiatives such as national art festivals play an important role in connecting artists’ creativity with sustainable livelihood opportunities and suggested that such festivals could be organized annually and strengthened through collaborations with various national forums and institutions to further expand their reach, impact, and visibility across the country.

Shri Jual Oram, Hon’ble Union Minister of Tribal Affairs, said, “India’s tribal communities possess an extraordinary wealth of art, culture, heritage, culinary traditions, and traditional knowledge systems that are truly unmatched. These living traditions reflect a deep connection with nature, community, and wisdom passed down through generations.

Taking forward the vision of our Hon’ble Prime Minister, we are making sustained efforts to create sustainable and aspirational livelihood opportunities for tribal communities. It is a matter of pride to witness the efforts made by the Ministry and collaborating organizations in bringing together artists from across the country and making this initiative truly successful. This is only the beginning, and we will continue to undertake such efforts in the future, aligned with the vision of our Hon’ble Prime Minister for a Viksit Bharat.”

During the ceremony, artists were felicitated for excellence and contribution to tribal art across five categories:

Best Artist in Tribal Art – Mr. Rajesh Chaitya Vangad

Young Achiever in Tribal Art – Mr. Dhaneshwar Dhurwey and Ms. Sudha Kumari

Innovation in Tribal Art – Ms. Laishram Membi Devi

Artist Reviving Tribal Art – Mr. Balasubramani

Lifetime Contribution to Tribal Art – Smt. Putli Ganju

During the exhibition, artists including Suresh Chandra Pungati, Rajesh Chaitya Vangad, Rajkumar Sodi, Mangala Bai, Bhuri Bai, Anil Chaitya Vangad, Kingson Swargiari, Ram Singh Urveti, Venkat Raman Singh Shyam, and Sukhnandi Vyam attracted significant buyers, with their artworks receiving strong public interest.

The festival also recorded a significant milestone in promoting tribal artists and their works, with over 800 artworks sold during the 12-day exhibition, generating direct sales worth more than ₹1.25 crore. The strong response from art enthusiasts, collectors, and visitors reflects the growing appreciation for tribal art and provides meaningful economic support to tribal artists while strengthening their visibility in the contemporary art landscape.

The programme concluded with a Vote of Thanks by Shri Anant Prakash Pandey, Joint Secretary, Ministry of Tribal Affairs, who commended the efforts made by all the collaborating partners, participating artists, and organizers for contributing to the success of the festival. He also expressed appreciation for the enthusiastic response from visitors, art enthusiasts, and collectors, which helped make the event a meaningful celebration of tribal art and culture.

headline: English rendering of PM’s speech during the launch of various development works in Guwahati, Assam
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**

The article is an English rendering of the Prime Minister's (PM) speech during the launch of various development works in Guwahati, Assam. The PM visited Guwahati on a holy site, Maa Kamakhya, and laid the foundation stone and inaugurated development projects worth 19.5 thousand crore rupees. The speech highlights the significance of the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme, which has deposited over 18 thousand crore rupees in the accounts of crores of farmers across the country. The speech also emphasizes the importance of self-reliance and the government's efforts to connect farmers with new technology, micro-irrigation, and solar pumps to reduce their expenditure on diesel.

**2. KEY FEATURES**

- **PM Kisan Samman Nidhi Scheme**: The scheme deposits money directly into the bank accounts of farmers with a click, a feature that even developed countries cannot replicate. Over four and a quarter lakh crore rupees have been deposited in the accounts of crores of farmers, with about 19 lakh farmers of Assam receiving nearly eight thousand crore rupees.
- **Self-reliance**: The government has promoted self-reliance in agriculture to protect farmers from external crises. It has encouraged the use of new technology, micro-irrigation, and solar pumps to reduce farmers' expenditure on diesel.
- **Micro-irrigation**: The government has implemented the 'Per Drop More Crop' policy, providing farmers with micro-irrigation technologies like drip and sprinklers, which have improved irrigation and reduced costs.
- **Kusum Scheme**: The government has launched the Kusum scheme to promote the use of solar pumps for irrigation, aiming to reduce farmers' expenditure on diesel.
- **MSP and cheap loans**: The government has increased the amount of money farmers receive in the form of MSP (Minimum Support Price) and provided cheap loans, which have become essential support for farmers.
- **Crop insurance**: The government has introduced crop insurance schemes to shield farmers from crop failures and other risks.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**

The speech highlights the significance of the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme as a social security net for small farmers and the importance of self-reliance in agriculture. The government's efforts to connect farmers with new technology, micro-irrigation, and solar pumps demonstrate its commitment to the welfare of farmers. The scheme's direct deposit of money into farmers' accounts shows the government's ability to bypass intermediaries and reach farmers directly.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**

This speech is highly relevant to the UPSC syllabus, particularly in the context of topics such as:

- **Agricultural Policies**: The speech highlights the government's policies to support farmers, including the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme, MSP, cheap loans, and crop insurance.
- **Self-reliance**: The government's efforts to promote self-reliance in agriculture are a key aspect of the country's development strategy.
- **Rural Development**: The speech emphasizes the importance of rural development and the role of the government in promoting the welfare of farmers and rural communities.
- **Economy**: The speech touches upon the government's efforts to support the economy through policies such as MSP, cheap loans, and crop insurance.

Analyzing the speech from a UPSC perspective requires the following steps:

1. Identifying the key features of the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme and the government's policies to support farmers.
2. Understanding the significance of self-reliance in agriculture and the government's efforts to promote it.
3. Analyzing the impact of the government's policies on rural development and the economy.
4. Evaluating the role of the government in promoting the welfare of farmers and rural communities.

**Conclusion**

In conclusion, the speech highlights the significance of the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme and the government's efforts to promote self-reliance in agriculture. The speech emphasizes the importance of supporting farmers and rural communities, which is a critical aspect of India's development strategy. From a UPSC perspective, the speech provides valuable insights into the government's policies and their impact on the economy, rural development, and the welfare of farmers.
fullText:
English rendering of PM’s speech during the launch of various development works in Guwahati, Assam

To my dear citizens of Guwahati, I offer you my heartfelt greetings.

Governor of Assam Lakshman Prasad Acharya Ji, popular Chief Minister of this place Himanta Biswa Sarma Ji, my colleague in the Union Cabinet Sarbananda Sonowal Ji, all present ministers of the State Government, representatives of the people, all our farmer companions connected to this program through technology from across the country, my brothers and sisters working in the tea gardens, and I see that many of our senior Chief Ministers are also connected with us today in this program from every corner of the country, my greetings to everyone.

Today, before Navratri, I have had the privilege of receiving your audience on this holy land of Maa Kamakhya. With the blessings of Maa Kamakhya, a short while ago, the foundation stone laying and inauguration of development projects worth 19.5 thousand crore rupees took place from here. From projects making Assam self-reliant in the energy sector to works done for the convenience of people coming to Assam, many projects have been given to Assam today. Today is also a very big day for the farmers of the country and companions working in the tea gardens in Assam. A few moments ago, across the entire country under the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi, an amount of more than 18 thousand crore rupees has been sent to the accounts of our crores of farmers. Apart from this, land titles have also been given today to many families associated with the tea gardens of Assam. I give many, many congratulations to the people of Assam, to all the families here, and to the farmers across the country.

Today farmer companions from across the country are connected to this land of Maa Kamakhya. A short while ago, messages of the PM Kisan Nidhi being deposited in the accounts of crores of farmers have reached everyone's mobile. This scheme is truly wonderful. With one click, money is deposited directly into the bank accounts of crores of farmers. Even in the developed countries of the world, such a thing is not possible. And when foreign guests meet me and ask so many questions on this subject and remain so curious for information, as to how all this is becoming possible. Even to the big leaders of the big countries of the world, this seems like a great miracle, and these are my same farmer brothers and sisters, most of whom before 2014 had neither a mobile phone nor any account in the bank. Today, in the accounts of such crores of farmers, more than four and a quarter lakh crore rupees have been deposited so far. In this, there are also about 19 lakh farmers of Assam, who have received nearly eight thousand crore rupees till now.

I remember, when the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme started, the Congress people, who are experts in spreading rumors and telling lies, used to say that although Modi gives the money for this scheme today, it will have to be returned after the elections; they are such experts in telling lies. But today this scheme of Samman Nidhi has become a medium of social security for the small farmers of the country.

For the BJP-NDA government, nothing is bigger than the interest of the farmers. When it came to giving money to farmers, the Congress people’s breath would get stuck. I give you another example. Before 2014, there was a Congress government at the Center for 10 years. In the 10 years of the Congress government, farmers received 6.5 lakh crore rupees in the form of MSP; 6.5 lakh crore in 10 years. Whereas in the 10 years of our government, farmers have received more than 20 lakh crore rupees in the form of MSP.

In the past 11 years, the BJP-NDA government has woven a strong security shield around the farmers of the country. Whether it is MSP, cheap loans, crop insurance, or PM Kisan Samman Nidhi, these schemes have become a great support for the farmers. Not only this, we have taken care that the impact of international crises does not fall on farming and agriculture. Now, as with Corona and the wars that happened after it, because of that, the prices of fertilizers in the international market had increased many times. It had also become difficult to get fertilizer in foreign markets. But the central government made full efforts to stop this crisis. The bag of urea that is available for 3 thousand rupees in the international market, our government has given that same bag to its farmers for only 300 rupees. On this, the government has spent more than 12 lakh crore rupees from its treasury, so that the burden does not fall on my farmers. The government is lifting this burden on its own shoulders.

In the past decade, the BJP-NDA government has done another very big work regarding self-reliance. We have encouraged self-reliance to protect agriculture from external crises. We have seen repeatedly in independent India, that when wars happen in other parts of the world, or difficulties arise in the supply chain, the trouble used to be for our farmer. Sometimes fertilizer would become expensive, sometimes the price of diesel and energy would increase. Because for decades, the Congress party kept the country dependent on other countries. Because of this, the cost of farming also used to increase.

With the intention that farmers' agriculture connects with new technology, they be connected to new methods of irrigation, and crops also get benefits, our government made the policy of 'Per Drop More Crop', and reached micro-irrigation technologies like drip and sprinklers to the farmers. Because of this, irrigation also improved and the cost also decreased. Today the government is working to connect your fields with solar pumps, and our effort is that your expenditure on diesel should be as low as possible. The Kusum scheme that the Center has made is for this reason. Today many farmers are not only doing irrigation by installing solar pumps but are also able to earn money by making electricity from it. And that is why I say, may the food-provider (Annadata) become the energy-provider (Urjadata).

It is our constant effort that the dependence of farmers on fertilizers and pesticides also reduces. Due to the wrong policies of Congress, five big fertilizer factories of the country had shut down. We got those fertilizer factories started again. Along with this, many works were also done to connect farmers with Nano Urea. Today the farmer of the country is getting its benefit. Now the BJP-NDA government is giving encouragement to the farmers of the country for natural farming. When farmers adopt natural farming on a very large scale, the soil will also remain safe and our food-providers will also remain safe from global crises.

On one side the BJP-NDA government is working regarding farmers, we are engaged in making the country self-reliant, while today Congress has once again proved that they are not honest towards the country in any situation. Even amidst the crises created by war today, Congress is engaged only in spreading rumors and counter-propaganda. And I want to say to the Congress companions, do one thing, listen to the speeches given by Pandit Nehru Ji from the Red Fort on August 15; you will be surprised to hear. Once from the Red Fort on August 15, Pandit Ji is saying that a war is going on in North Korea and South Korea, therefore inflation is increasing in India. Now where is North Korea, South Korea and Nehru Ji was talking about inflation increasing here. And today the people of Congress are busy misleading the country. What the impact of global crises is, the world is seeing today.

The BJP-NDA government has worked for the development of our refineries and for the expansion of refining capacity. Today India is that country of the world which can take care of not just its own, but the world's energy needs as well. Our refineries here in Assam have also been expanded in the past years. Unprecedented investment is being made in gas pipeline infrastructure in the entire North East including Assam. The work of upgrading the Numaligarh-Siliguri pipeline has also been completed. And the world's first second-generation bio-ethanol plant has also been established in Golaghat, Assam. This whole region will have a very big benefit from all these projects.

In order for the railway's dependence on foreign countries to reduce, and so that we have to order less oil from abroad, a very big work has happened in a decade. In the last ten years, today nearly the entire rail network of the country has been connected with electric routes. And soon, we are also completing the goal of 100 percent electrification. Due to this electrification, about one and three-quarter billion liters of diesel is being saved in the country. In Assam too, electrification of the railway network is happening rapidly. Today also, the inauguration and foundation stone laying of projects related to the electrification of railways have taken place here.

Assam is also playing a big role in the fulfillment of resolutions related to renewable energy in the country. The entire North East including Assam will benefit from the Lower Kopili Hydro Power Project. And the biggest thing, these projects worth thousands of crore rupees that have started in Assam, they are creating new opportunities for employment for the youth of Assam, and are increasing the prosperity of Assam.

The identity of the BJP-NDA double engine government is with sensitivity and with good governance. The development of every region and every class of the country is our priority. With this same sentiment, a very big initiative has happened today for the companions working in the tea gardens of Assam. Those by whose hard work the global identity of Assam was strengthened, those by whose grown tea's fragrance India's identity was established across the world, today the BJP-NDA government is giving both respect and assistance to those companions. The Congress governments did not even ask about these companions; they did not even have legal documents for the land. I will congratulate Himanta Ji and his entire team; you have done a very big work for these families associated with tea gardens. Today the government of Assam is doing the work of ending this historical injustice. Now these families have received their own land, and the way to get permanent houses for them has also become clear. From this, sisters living in tea gardens will especially benefit a lot. I would want that now the children of families working in tea gardens also study well and move forward. And the BJP government has created full facilities for this as well. That is why every tea garden worker is saying—Once again, BJP government! And for me, to respect these laborers of my tea garden means I am myself repaying a debt, because while you used to work in the gardens, that tea leaf used to reach my village in far-off Gujarat, and I reached here by selling tea. Now tell me, if there were no blessings of these tea garden people, would I have reached here? The tea of these tea garden people reached my place and today I reached among you. And see what kind of grace of Maa Kamakhya it is, that today Maa Kamakhya Devi has given me the opportunity to repay the debt of my brothers of the tea garden.

From today, the work of building modern cruise terminals at the famous Nimati Ghat and Bishwanath Ghat has also started. This is not just an infrastructure project; this is a work that gives a new direction to tourism and the local economy in Assam. The BJP government has not limited tourism only to sightseeing spots, but has seen it as a big opportunity for employment and development. With this same thought, possibilities of water tourism are being increased in the Brahmaputra River too. With the construction of the cruise terminal, the work of running cruises on the Brahmaputra will move forward and it will be easier for tourists from the country and the world to reach Assam. When cruise tourism increases, new opportunities for employment will be created for local youth. People associated with artisans and handicrafts will get a new market. The income of small shopkeepers, boatmen, and people associated with hotels and transport will also increase. That is, tourism in Assam is now not limited only to traveling around; it is becoming a new engine of local development and public prosperity.

Assam today is becoming a model of the new future of our North East, i.e., Ashtalakshmi. The progress here is giving new momentum to the entire North East. The change that the BJP-NDA government has brought in Assam in the past decade, its impact is also visible in neighboring West Bengal. There too, people want similar good governance of BJP-NDA. Therefore, I will say to every youth and every family of Assam, that we have to keep working together for a developed Assam. So that Assam emerges as a model state in the country. With this same urge, while all the Honorable Chief Ministers are connected with us in this ceremony, I want to make an urge to them—the PM Ekta Mall that has been inaugurated here, you also make arrangements to sell things of your state here. You take some space here, and if someone in Assam wants something from Kerala, it should be available right from here; if some thing of Maharashtra is wanted, it should be available right from here; if some thing of Gujarat is wanted, it should be available from here; if that of Rajasthan is wanted, it should be available here; meaning there should be a vision of the unity of the entire country. Every state of India should have a presence in this mall; every district of Assam should have a presence in this mall. One District, One Product; meaning in a way, if any person goes to the Ekta Mall, they should find the whole of India there. And I am confident that the entire country will get a model from the land of Assam and these Ekta Malls will be successfully prepared in every state of the country. I once again give you many, many best wishes for the development projects. I also again express much gratitude to the farmer companions who have been connected with us from across the country, and I pray to Maa Kamakhya that when Navratri begins after a few days, the grace of Maa Kamakhya remains on all countrymen and we move forward with new faith and new strength, crossing all crises; my many, many best wishes.

Read this release in: Urdu , हिन्दी , Gujarati

headline: TRAI releases “Draft Telecom Commercial Communication Preference (Third Amendment) Regulations, 2026” for Consultation
summaryText:
Ministry of Communications

TRAI releases “Draft Telecom Commercial Communication Preference (Third Amendment) Regulations, 2026” for Consultation

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) today issued the draft “Telecom Commercial Communications Customer Preference (Third Amendment) Regulations, 2026, for consultation.

To curb the menace of Unsolicited Commercial Communications (UCC). TRAI issued Telecom Commercial Communications Customer Preference Regulations, 2018 (TCCCPR,2018) on 19th July, 2018, which put in place a framework for regulating Commercial Communication. The TCCCPR, 2018 was amended on 21st December, 2018 and subsequently, second amendment was issued on 12th February 2025.

Based on the feedback from stakeholders during various interactions well as in light of recent developments like implementation of AI-based detection of UCC, by major access providers, a need has been felt to make amendments in some of the existing provisions, and also to add some new provisions to make the regulatory framework more effective and efficient.

The Draft Telecom Commercial Communication Preference (Third Amendment) Regulations, 2026, has been placed on TRAI's website www.trai.gov.in, seeking comments from the stakeholders. The written comments on the issues raised in the Draft amendment, and counter comments, if any, may be sent preferably by mail to advqos@trai.gov.in, in the specified format, by 12th April, 2026, and 27th April, 2026, respectively.

For any clarification/ information, Shri Deepak Sharma, Advisor (QoS-2), may be contacted at advqos@trai.gov.in or +91-11-20907760.

Read this release in: हिन्दी

headline: National Additive Manufacturing Symposium (NAMS) 2026: Driving Digital Manufacturing through Additive Manufacturing
summaryText:
**National Additive Manufacturing Symposium (NAMS) 2026: Driving Digital Manufacturing through Additive Manufacturing**

**1. CONTEXT**
The Ministry of Electronics & IT (MeitY) organized the second National Additive Manufacturing Symposium (NAMS) on 13th March 2026 at Scope Convention Centre, New Delhi. The event aimed to promote the growth of the Additive Manufacturing ecosystem in India by bringing together policymakers, industry leaders, researchers, start-ups, and stakeholders.

**2. KEY FEATURES**
- The symposium served as a national platform for discussions on policy directions, technological advancements, market opportunities, and ecosystem strengthening for accelerating additive manufacturing adoption in key sectors of the Indian economy.
- Distinguished dignitaries inaugurated the event, including Shri S. Krishnan, Secretary, MeitY, and Lt. Gen. Rajiv Kumar Sahni, Director General of EME.
- The "India and Global Additive Manufacturing Ecosystem 2026" report was released, along with exchange of MoUs, AM product launches, and the announcement of a Grand Challenge winner.
- Panel discussions focused on policy initiatives under NSAM 2.0, defence adoption, industry perspectives, MeitY-supported R&D centres, and strategic R&D collaboration across ministries and stakeholders.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**
- Additive Manufacturing is recognized as a sunrise sector integrating digital technologies with physical manufacturing to strengthen India's digital economy.
- The government is supporting technology development through various strategies and incentives, including machines for additive manufacturing, materials, and products.
- The symposium witnessed participation from multiple ministries, departments, and state governments, reflecting growing inter-ministerial interest in additive manufacturing.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**
- Additive Manufacturing is a significant aspect of the 'Make in India' initiative, and its growth can contribute to India's economic development.
- The government's support for additive manufacturing can be a crucial factor in promoting digital manufacturing in the country.
- The symposium's focus on policy initiatives, technological advancements, and ecosystem strengthening can be related to the UPSC syllabus on Economic Development, Industry, and Technology Policy.
- This event can be used to discuss the role of the government in promoting innovation and entrepreneurship in the country.
fullText:
Ministry of Electronics & IT

National Additive Manufacturing Symposium (NAMS) 2026: Driving Digital Manufacturing through Additive Manufacturing

The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, organized the second edition of the National Additive Manufacturing Symposium (NAMS) on 13th March 2026 at Scope Convention Centre, New Delhi, bringing together policymakers, industry leaders, researchers, start-ups and other stakeholders to deliberate on the growth of the Additive Manufacturing (AM) ecosystem in India. The symposium served as a national platform for discussions on policy directions, technological advancements, market opportunities and ecosystem strengthening required to accelerate the adoption of additive manufacturing across key sectors of the Indian economy.

The event was inaugurated by distinguished dignitaries including Shri S. Krishnan, Secretary, MeitY; Shri Pratyaya Amrit, Chief Secretary, Government of Bihar; Lt. Gen. Rajiv Kumar Sahni, Director General of EME and Colonel Commandant, Indian Army; Shri Amitesh Kumar Sinha, Additional Secretary, MeitY; Shri Anurag Yadav, Principal Secretary IT&E, Government of Uttar Pradesh; Shri Bharath Reddy, Joint Director, ITE&C Department, Government of Telangana; and Smt. Sunita Verma, Scientist-G and Group Coordinator, R&D Electronics, MeitY.

Shri S. Krishnan, Secretary, MeitY stated “Additive Manufacturing is emerging as a sunrise sector where digital technologies integrate with physical manufacturing to strengthen India’s digital economy. He noted the significant progress since NAMS 2024 and highlighted the importance of building domestic capabilities in electronics components, capital goods and advanced materials, supported by collaborative initiatives across government, industry and academia.” Additive Manufacturing is playing vital role in India’s manufacturing economy and government is supporting technology development - machines for additive manufacturing, materials and products through various strategies and incentives.

During the inaugural session, the “India and Global Additive Manufacturing Ecosystem 2026” report was released, along with exchange of MoUs, AM product launches and the announcement of a Grand Challenge winner. The symposium featured panel discussions on policy initiatives under NSAM 2.0, defence adoption, industry perspectives, the role of MeitY-supported R&D centres, and strategic R&D collaboration across ministries and stakeholders. The event reaffirmed the Government’s commitment to advancing digital manufacturing.

The symposium witnessed participation from officials representing multiple ministries, departments and state governments, reflecting growing inter-ministerial interest in additive manufacturing. Participants included representatives from the Department of Space, Ministry of Defence, Department of Atomic Energy, Ministry of Food Processing Industries, Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Ministry of New and Renewable Energy, and the State Governments of Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Odisha and Telangana. Experts from institutions such as the Indian Army’s Corps of Electronics and Mechanical Engineers (EME), VSSC, RR-CAT, NIFTEM, IIT Delhi and Ajay Kumar Garg Engineering College (AKGEC), along with industry representatives from STPL, Intech Additive Solutions, Stratasys, Solize Partners India, Sara Infoways, Pandaav Applications and Philips Additive and associations including ELCINA, ICEA, AMSI and 3D Graphy, also participated.
